title: Reasoning Enhancement
subtitle: Reasoning Enhancement
description: Extended Thinking, hierarchical LLM strategies, and MoE routing to enhance Agent reasoning capabilities and efficiency. Implements structured reasoning processes with verification and cost optimization through selective enablement based on task complexity.
sub_problems: Extended Thinking: Leverage model deep thinking capabilities
Hierarchical LLM: Use small models for simple tasks, large models for complex tasks
Chain-of-Thought: Guide models through step-by-step reasoning
MoE Routing: Route tasks to expert models based on task type
Reasoning Verification: Validate correctness of reasoning process
Dual-Loop Decoupling: Separate evidence collection and reasoning solving into independent loops
Reasoning Chain Persistence: Record reasoning process as auditable step chains
Selective Precise Answers: Decide whether to generate precise concise answers based on question type
best_practices: Selective reasoning enablement: Generation tasks don't need deep reasoning, analysis tasks do
Three-tier budget allocation: Allocate thinking budget by task complexity to avoid token waste
Outline as living document: New information from subsequent searches should feed back to outline structure
XML structured output with fallback: Prioritize parsing structured tags with degradation strategy on failure
Prompt enhancement as independent subgraph: Decouple from main workflow for independent testing and reuse
Analysis loop with explicit stop conditions: Stop when tool plan is empty or returns none
Solve steps should reference analysis phase: Use available_cite to bridge two loops
Memory system should support version compatibility: Implement load_or_create pattern for checkpoint resumption

values.MiroThinker: Extended Thinking internal reasoning chain within budget_tokens
values.DeepTutor: Dual-Loop: Analysis Loop collects evidence + Solve Loop step-by-step reasoning
Unified model, BaseAgent parameter configuration via agents.yaml
Configurable max_iterations and max_corrections to control call count

values.DeepResearch: Deep research with continuous outline evolution
values.DeepTutor: Academic QA, complex multi-step reasoning, scenarios requiring citation tracking
Multi-turn iterative: N analysis loop rounds + M corrections per solve loop step
values.PageIndex: Thinking-First JSON — each LLM prompt forces thinking reasoning field before conclusion

values.DeepTutor: SolveChainStep chain: each step contains target + tool_calls + response
values.PageIndex: Unified {thinking, answer_field} JSON schema with extract_json fault-tolerant parsing

values.DeepTutor: Analysis-solve decoupling + knowledge chain summarization + two-stage precise answers
values.PageIndex: Tree search reasoning + verify-fix-retry loop + three-mode progressive degradation

values.DeepTutor: Configuration-driven iteration limits + selective PrecisionAnswer enablement
values.PageIndex: asyncio.gather concurrent batch calls + recursive large node subdivision to reduce per-call token consumption

values.DeepTutor: Multi-round RAG in Analysis Loop + hybrid Web Search retrieval
values.PageIndex: Vectorless — use LLM reasoning to replace vector similarity, navigate tree structure for location
No explicit token budget, implicitly constrain reasoning length via temperature=0 + structured JSON
Bottom-up: TOC detection → structure extraction → page mapping → large node recursive subdivision
Inject Expert Knowledge directly into tree search prompt, no embedding fine-tuning needed

solutions.title: reasoning_mcp_server.py Extended Thinking with Three-Tier Budget
solutions.description: reasoning_mcp_server.py (63 lines only) exposes reasoning() tool via @mcp.tool(), calling Anthropic API with thinking={"type": "enabled", "budget_tokens": 19000} to enable Extended Thinking mode. ReasoningService encapsulates three-tier thinking budgets: light(8K tokens for QuestionerAgent evaluation), medium(16K for Planner planning), heavy(24K for FactCheck verification). Selective enablement strategy: Writer/Coder/Artist use normal mode (generation tasks don't need reasoning), Planner/Reviewer/FactCheck/ThreadChecker use Thinking mode (analysis tasks need reasoning). Incremental cost only +$0.20-0.30 per article with high ROI.

solutions.title: WebWeaver Dynamic Outline Optimization
solutions.description: Based on WebWeaver paper (arXiv 2509.13312) Dynamic Outline Optimization concept: outline is not one-time product but living document evolving with information collection. Planner Agent alternates Web search and outline revision in continuous loop, evaluating new information impact on existing outline each round, supporting section add/delete/merge/split and reordering. Search-Outline Interleaving creates positive feedback: outline v1 → identify gaps → search → new discoveries → outline v2 → ... Writer Agent uses Memory-Grounded Hierarchical Synthesis, retrieving most relevant evidence subset from memory for each section. Concept-level solution without source code migration.

solutions.title: prompt_enhancer LangGraph Subgraph with XML Structured Output Parsing
solutions.description: DeerFlow's prompt_enhancer is independent LangGraph subgraph with three-file hierarchy: PromptEnhancerState(TypedDict, 4 fields: prompt/context/report_style/output) → prompt_enhancer_node() core enhancement logic (get_llm_by_type + apply_prompt_template Jinja2 template + XML <enhanced_prompt> tag parsing + fallback prefix removal) → build_graph() graph construction. Exposed via /api/enhance_prompt FastAPI endpoint. Supports 5 ReportStyle types (ACADEMIC/POPULAR_SCIENCE/NEWS/SOCIAL_MEDIA/STRATEGIC_INVESTMENT) and locale internationalization injection.

solutions.title: Loop Reasoning Enhancement Solution
solutions.description: Decouple analysis and solving through multi-loop architecture to achieve traceable structured reasoning process

solutions.title: CoT Research Reasoning Solution
solutions.description: When Agent executes research tasks, directly searching user's original query often yields shallow results

solutions.title: Reasoning-Based Retrieval Alternative to Vector Search
solutions.description: Traditional RAG systems rely on vector similarity search (embedding + cosine similarity) for document retrieval, but similarity ≠ relevance. For professional long documents (financial reports, regulations, academic papers), vector search has three fundamental limitations
